expectations.text: Uses findings from the current repository and current feature-dev skill rather than generic advice.
expectations.evidence: Cites `skills/feature-dev/SKILL.md` directly, quotes the frontmatter description ('medium-to-large changes in unfamiliar codebases'), names Phases 2/3/5/6 by number, and quotes all four existing code-explorer prompt templates verbatim.

expectations.text: Asks targeted clarifying questions that are grounded in concrete risks or ambiguities.
expectations.evidence: Each question is anchored to a specific risk: Q1 to the ambiguity between Deep track and product-strategy framing; Q2 to three named failure modes (skipped orientation, wrong question level, over-assumed context); Q3 to the material difference between dev-setup orientation and product-context orientation.

expectations.text: Caps clarifying questions to a small, prioritized set rather than turning discovery into a broad interview.
expectations.evidence: Exactly three questions, introduced as 'Three questions that would materially change the scope of each proposed change.'

expectations.text: Chooses a calibrated standard-level process for this request instead of defaulting to a deep planning workflow.
expectations.evidence: Opens with '**Track**: Standard' and explicitly explains why Deep is not warranted: 'the scope is bounded and the skill is well-structured; this doesn't need broad multi-angle exploration.'

expectations.text: Separates current understanding, open questions, and a recommended approach.
expectations.evidence: Response has distinct top-level sections: 'Understanding', 'Relevant Findings', 'Open Questions', and 'Recommendation'.

expectations.text: Explains why the proposed changes would help with unfamiliar repositories.
expectations.evidence: Each of the three proposed changes includes a 'Why this helps unfamiliar repos specifically' sub-section (e.g., 'Orientation anchors downstream agents in the actual dev environment rather than an assumed one.').

expectations.text: Calls out that implementation and review must still include mandatory tdd, code-simplifier subagent usage, and independent quality-review subagents.
expectations.evidence: 'What to Keep Unchanged' section names all three explicitly: 'TDD in Phase 5 — already mandatory on every track with explicit red-green-refactor loop', 'code-simplifier subagent in Phase 6 — already required', 'Independent code-reviewer subagents in Phase 6 — already required on every track.'
